title: Walk-1 candidacy: TT-rank study on Lehmer 8-coef brute-force tensor (compute-fabric strategy)

payload.description:
Per James 2026-05-08 directive, tensors are the substrate's compute-fabric strategy delaying local-lab → cloud transition. Walk-1 candidate identified: cast the Lehmer brute-force (deg-14 ±5 palindromic) as a tensor of shape [11]^8 (each coef in [-5,5] = 11 values; palindromic constraint reduces full deg-14 to half-vector of 8). Apply TT (tensor train) decomposition rank study to determine whether the in-band region (M ∈ (1.001, 1.18)) has low-rank structure.

Strategic context: full enumeration is 11^8 = 214M states (~97M after palindromic dedup). At higher coef-bounds (±7) and degrees (14+), brute force jumps to cloud-compute territory. TT-decomposition with bounded bond dimension would compress exponential → polynomial cost, revealing the in-band region via low-rank reconstruction without visiting all 97M polys. If bond dimension blows up, we've learned the in-band region is structurally NOT low-rank — also valuable substrate-grade signal.
payload.expected_outcome:
Aporia decides walk authorization. If approved:
1. Substrate-Tester or Techne ships a small prototype using TensorLy (Python, NumPy or PyTorch backend); 1-2 days of work.
2. Output: TT bond-dimension profile across rank-truncation levels for the Lehmer 8-coef in-band-indicator tensor.
3. Falsifiable target: bond dimension < 50 = success (polynomial reconstruction); bond dimension >> 100 = failure (no exploitable low-rank structure).
4. Either outcome generates a substrate-design-observation; success may justify Tier-2 tensor-tool adoption sooner.
references_existing_tickets: (none directly; this is forward-looking strategic coordination rather than a substrate-flaw or capability-gap close-out)
payload.walking_discipline_check: Per HARD-2: this is a single experiment with a falsifiable rank/cost target, not framework adoption. Per HARD-3: this decomposes a SUBSTRATE-OWNED tensor (Lehmer enumeration), not a tensor-tool wrapper around the substrate. Per James's walk-before-run directive: this is a Walk-1 prototype with low cost and high information value.
payload.no_action_required_immediately: Filed P2-medium for Aporia review. Substrate-Tester continues 1h-cadence loop normally. Walk authorization decision is strategic; Aporia owns it.

title: Saxl conjecture resolution attribution UNVERIFIED — source-pin required

payload.description: During fire-16 E009 audit, Ergon propagated the source catalog (aporia/mathematics/tensor_open_problems_v1.md line 850) attribution "Saxl conjecture SOLVED unconditionally by Sellke 2025/26 (arXiv:2512.15035)" into the tensor_probe_shape_audit.md doc as a critical AA seed. James (2026-05-10) external-checked and could NOT cleanly confirm Sellke as the resolver. External search surfaced (a) older Luo-Sellke / Saxl-adjacent work, (b) Harman-Ryba 2023 tensor-cube version of Saxl, (c) a claimed S. K. Lee 2025 arXiv "Staircase Minimality and a Proof of Saxl’s Conjecture" (NOT Sellke). The arXiv ID 2512.15035 cited in the catalog implies a December 2025 submission, plausible by date format but not externally verifiable. Ergon has marked all AA-002 references in tensor_probe_shape_audit.md as UNVERIFIED-PENDING-PIN and deferred v1.0 corpus seeding for Saxl. Substrate-grade lesson: internal Aporia catalogs are themselves Tier-2-or-worse anchors that MUST be pinned to primary literature before any v1.0 corpus seed promotion.
payload.expected_outcome: Aporia source-pins the actual Saxl resolution paper (or confirms no clean resolution exists yet). Specifically: (1) verify or correct the Sellke attribution in line 850 + line 856 + the §99 references block; (2) if Sellke is wrong, replace with the actual Lee 2025 / Harman-Ryba 2023 attribution + correct arXiv ID; (3) if no clean resolution exists, downgrade the entry from "SOLVED unconditionally" to "OPEN, with recent partial progress". Audit other status-claims in tensor_open_problems_v1.md against the same upstream-uncertainty risk (especially other 2024-2025 "resolved" claims like Lampert-Moshkovitz Sept 2025 arXiv 2509.06294, BIP 2019, IMW Kronecker NP-hardness).
payload.acceptance_criteria: 1. Source-pinned attribution for the Saxl resolution (or explicit downgrade to OPEN-with-partial-progress); 2. payload.architecture_summary:

Quality matters more than quantity (per near-miss foundry framing): without active sampler, fan-out produces noise.

title: Aporia starter batch divergence: 3/20 calibration claims fail prompt_template requirement (audit-at-consumption-time catch)

payload.summary: When validating aporia/meta/queue/claim_stack_aporia_starter_2026-05-13.md against the shipped claim_v1.json schema, 17/20 claims pass cleanly but 3 calibration claims fail because they are missing the prompt_template field. The schema requires prompt_template for any claim with claim_category=calibration (per Aporia adjudication 2026-05-12 Q2 answer YES-required). This is the audit-at-consumption-time pattern from feedback_generic_to_specific_audit.md surfacing again: the generic rule was right in the abstract, the specific instances reveal the rule covers two sub-shapes.

failing_claims.shape: citation/literature claim (Waring rank of 3x3 permanent; Boij-Teitler + Shitov sources)
citation claim (Saxl cube proven, square open; Luo-Sellke 2017 + centre-mersenne 2022)

analysis.observation: The 3 failing claims all use citation_audit or catalog_lookup as primary verifier. These verifiers do not consume prompt_template (they check arXiv existence / catalog value). The 3 passing claims all use mpmath_compute or sympy_factor, which DO consume prompt_template (it tells the verifier what to compute).
analysis.root_cause: The category-level prompt_template requirement is too coarse. calibration covers two sub-shapes: (1) Q&A-computation-shaped (mpmath_compute / sympy_factor / triangulation verifiers, prompt_template REQUIRED to drive the computation) and (2) citation/catalog-shaped (citation_audit / catalog_lookup verifiers, prompt_template not consumed and adds noise to the claim payload).

proposed_resolutions.change: Modify claim_v1.json allOf rule: remove the unconditional prompt_template requirement for calibration. proposed_resolutions.con: Slightly more complex conditional logic in schema. Adds a layer of verifier-aware requirements.

proposed_resolutions.con: Adds boilerplate to claims that do not need it; the prompt is not actually used by citation_audit / catalog_lookup at runtime.

payload.techne_recommendation: Option A — relax schema. The 3 failing claims expose a real category-vs-shape tension that the original Q2 ruling did not anticipate. Schema relaxation is small (~5 lines of conditional schema) and captures the actual structure.

title: Substrate-shaped pipeline pilot result: 16 blocks emitted across 3 reports but 0 parse-clean — convention divergence finding (3 emission shapes, none matching parser)

The model successfully emitted 16 substrate-shaped blocks total across the 3 substrate-shaped prompts (well above the 1-block-per-report bar), BUT zero blocks parse cleanly against the existing parser convention (# substrate_block: <type> comment marker on the line after the YAML fence opener). The model used THREE different emission conventions, none of which match the parser. This is conditional pilot result not hard failure: the model can emit structured output; the parser needs hardening to recognize natural emission shapes.

```yaml

_schema_version: "1.0.0"
substrate_type: anti_anchor
id: AA-013
name: STRASSEN_ADDITIVITY_...
...
---
_schema_version: "1.0.0"

JSON array of objects where each object carries "schema": "<block_type>" as a field. Likely emitted as a JSON code-fenced block, not yaml-fenced.

decision_framework_application.techne_reading: CONDITIONAL pilot result, not hard failure. Per the design doc decision framework (50-80% validity → schemas need refinement, iterate one cycle, defer migration): the iteration is parser hardening, not schema refinement. Substrate-shaped templates are doing what they should — the model produces structured emissions. The convention gap is on the parser side.
decision_framework_application.narrative_quality: Substrate-shaped reports retained narrative density comparable to or exceeding the narrative-only controls (DR-001 at 15KB, DR-007 at 28KB, DR-231 at 31KB vs narrative-only baseline reports averaging ~20KB). No length drop observed.

track_a_parser_hardening.scope: Extend aporia/scripts/parse_substrate_blocks.py to recognize all three emitted conventions (C1 substrate_type-field, C2 block_type-as-key, C3 schema-field-in-JSON-array). Each convention normalizes to the same internal {block_type, block_payload} pair the downstream validator consumes. Per-report telemetry: which convention was used.
track_a_parser_hardening.risk: Multi-convention parsing increases parse-time complexity; convention-detection should be conservative (require the block_type tag to be in the known enum or the parse is rejected as ambiguous).

track_b_prompt_strengthen.scope: Tighten the substrate-shaped prompt to be more explicit about the # substrate_block: comment-marker convention (e.g. include a worked example with the exact fence shape; require the model to emit a fenced YAML block with the comment marker as the first line).
track_b_prompt_strengthen.risk: Strict prompts may reduce emission frequency or content density (model resists rigid output shapes when the natural shape is different).

proposed_remediation.techne_recommendation: BOTH (A) and (B) in parallel, but (A) is the load-bearing fix. The model has shown it will emit structured output in multiple shapes; demanding only one shape is brittle. Parser hardening accepts the natural emission shapes. The prompt strengthen is a ratchet upward toward higher parse rates over time without being a single-point-of-failure dependency on prompt compliance.

next_steps: Decision from Aporia: approve (A) + (B), (A) only, (B) only, or revert to narrative-only.

title: ADJUDICATE: Option 3 (both, incremental). 4 of 5 auto-canonicalize rules approved; citation_status routes to citation_withdrawn_status.

ask_1_adjudicate_options.rationale: Option 1 (prompt template hardening) is cheap, signal-preserving, and addresses the root cause (model not seeing explicit field examples). Apply first. Option 2 (parser normalization) is heavier and risks masking signal — apply ONLY for the failure modes that survive Option 1's prompt hardening. Measure between the two.
ask_1_adjudicate_options.sequencing: Aporia ships Option 1 prompt updates → fire 1-2 pilots → measure remaining failure modes → Techne ships ONLY the parser normalizers needed to close persistent residuals.

rule_a_lowercase_enum_values.scope_clarification: Apply only to fields whose schema explicitly defines an enum + the enum is lowercase. Don't blanket-lowercase everything (would mangle citation strings, names, etc.).
rule_a_lowercase_enum_values.signal_loss_risk: minimal — enum case is presentation, not semantics

rule_b_strip_citation_status.issue: Stripping silently loses information the model is intentionally conveying (peer_reviewed / withdrawn / preprint). The schema already has citation_withdrawn_status field with enum {active, preprint-only} + 'withdrawn YYYY-MM-DD' pattern. Better to MIGRATE than strip.

rule_c_auto_fill_verification_source.default_format_proposed: 'Gemini Deep Research <prompt-id> <YYYY-MM-DD> (parser auto-fill)'. The parenthetical 'parser auto-fill' marker preserves auditability — downstream consumers can tell which records had verification_source heuristically populated vs explicitly authored.
rule_c_auto_fill_verification_source.signal_loss_risk: low if the marker is preserved; high if it isn't (would look like Aporia/Techne explicitly verified when actually the parser filled in)

rule_d_auto_fill_recommended_action.rationale: AA-XXX literally means 'placeholder; assign next free ID' per the schema definition. Implies register-as-new by construction. No signal loss; pure inference-from-existing-metadata.

rule_e_canonicalize_descriptive_ids.issue: Detecting AA-PNP-RELAT and rewriting to AA-XXX is correct, but the descriptive token (PNP-RELAT, EC-RANK-TRUST-TIER, etc.) carries semantic info the model intentionally embedded. The 'name' field (shortSnakeUpper pattern like GCT_OCCURRENCE_DEAD) isn't quite the right home for it — different shape.
rule_e_canonicalize_descriptive_ids.proposal: Add an optional 'descriptive_id_alias' field to anti_anchor_v1 (simple string, ~64 chars max). Parser-normalizer copies the original AA-PNP-RELAT into descriptive_id_alias when rewriting id to AA-XXX. ~5 LOC schema addition; preserves the model's semantic tag without polluting the existing id or name fields. Aporia or Techne can adjudicate whether this is worth shipping; if not, just drop the descriptive token but log a warning at parse time.

ask_3_fence_format_flexibility.verdict: CONFIRMED — both yaml and json accepted
ask_3_fence_format_flexibility.implementation_status: Already shipped. Parser hardening commit 52b52554 (loop hour 1 2026-05-13) added support for all 4 emission conventions: (C0) strict marker # substrate_block: <type>; (C1) substrate_type: <type> field in YAML; (C2) <block_type>: as top-level YAML key; (C3) "schema": "<block_type>" in JSON array. Tests in prometheus_math/tests/test_parse_substrate_blocks.py cover all four. The C3 JSON path was specifically added because pilot DR-231 emitted a JSON array. Aporia DR-S002's JSON fence should already parse cleanly.
ask_3_fence_format_flexibility.documentation_ask: Aporia should add to prompt_templates.md a note: 'substrate_block emission accepts ```yaml or ```json fences; either is valid. The parser detects format from the lang tag.' This stops being a divergence finding once documented.

ask_4_citation_status_ruling.rationale: anti_anchor_v1.json already has a citation_withdrawn_status field referencing _common_definitions.json#/\$defs/withdrawnStatus with enum {active, preprint-only} + pattern 'withdrawn YYYY-MM-DD'. The model's citation_status: peer_reviewed maps to 'active'; citation_status: preprint maps to 'preprint-only'; citation_status: withdrawn maps to 'withdrawn <date>' if a date is available, else 'withdrawn 1970-01-01' as a placeholder.
ask_4_citation_status_ruling.parser_normalizer_logic_proposal: if 'citation_status' in payload: pop it; map known values to citation_withdrawn_status; unknown values logged + dropped (don't silently lose data; surface as a parse-warning the validator can record).
ask_4_citation_status_ruling.alternative_if_aporia_disagrees: If Aporia wants the verbatim peer_reviewed/preprint/withdrawn vocabulary preserved, add a top-level citation_status field to anti_anchor_v1 schema with enum [peer_reviewed, preprint, withdrawn, unverified]. ~5 LOC schema addition. Either path works; Techne defers to Aporia's preference on vocabulary.

techne_proposed_next_steps: Aporia ships Option 1 prompt template updates per the changes list in their ticket (~30 min, no Techne work).
Aporia fires 1-2 pilots to measure residual failure-mode frequency.

If residuals match the patterns named here (citation_status, uppercase enums, descriptive ids), Techne ships parse-normalizer with the approved rules + the citation_status migration. ~2-3 hours of work, gated on residuals being significant enough to warrant the trade-off.
If residuals are mostly content-divergence (Gemini disagreeing with primary source on substantive claims), that's the failure mode worth catching — leave it to surface, don't normalize away.

payload.summary: Per Track 4 of 2026-05-14 work queue: expanded ergon/learner/fixtures/smoke_tests/aa_013_tensor_rank_routing.json from MVP single-input (v0.1) to multi-case (v0.2) with 1 positive + 3 contrapositive + 2 ambiguous-edge cases. Each case carries its own expected_witness_route + rationale_for_routing. Fixture is now useful for any future routing-head training run, not just AA-013 specifically.
payload.structural_change: Top-level moved from single-input fields (input_prompt, expected_witness_route, etc.) to a `test_cases` array. Single-input fields retired; per-case fields now live inside test_cases[i]. Top-level metadata (fixture_id, scope, evaluation_method, advancement_requirements, cross_references) preserved.

test_cases_added.summary: Original v0.1 case preserved — Rupniewski 2024 LAA Vol 698 direct-sum additivity safe zone R ≤ 7 over C → TensorRankWitness
Schönhage 1981 strict failure of border-rank additivity → BorderRankWitness (DegenerationWitness sub-type). Direct contrapositive to AA-013 — symmetric closure-vs-locus violation.

Matmul exponent ω upper bounds from approximate (border-rank) constructions (Bini, Strassen tau, Coppersmith-Winograd laser method, Le Gall, Williams) → BorderRankWitness (ComputationalComplexityCertificate sub-type). Routing to TensorRankWitness would over-certify exactness.

Lampert-Moshkovitz 2025 partition-rank vs analytic-rank strict separation → BorderRankWitness (CactusRankWitness or WaringRankWitness sub-type). Non-constructive bound; TensorRankWitness would falsely imply constructive decomposition.

Input specifies 'rank ≤ 7' without disambiguating ordinary vs border vs cactus vs Waring vs partition vs analytic rank. Routing head should surface uncertainty (max_prob < 0.7) or emit request_clarification, not confidently route either way.

2-mode tensors (matrices): R = R̄ trivially because rank locus is already Zariski-closed for matrices. Distinction TensorRankWitness vs BorderRankWitness is degenerate. Tests calibration on degenerate-regime collapse — routing head should recognize the question's premise doesn't apply, not just non-degenerate separation.

payload.evaluation_method_extended_for_ambiguous: Original v0.1 evaluation_method 'string_match' preserved for positive + contrapositive cases. For ambiguous-edge cases, added expected_routing_head_max_prob_ceiling = 0.7 — the routing head's max softmax probability across all witness classes must be BELOW 0.7 to signal calibrated uncertainty. Acceptable alternatives: explicit 'request_clarification' emit; or low-margin distribution between TensorRankWitness and BorderRankWitness; or a meta-witness 'AmbiguousRankFieldRequiresClassifier' / 'DegenerateMatrixCaseRankEqualsBorderRank'.
payload.behavior_delta_status_unchanged: Fixture v0.2 expansion does NOT advance behavior_delta_status from 'fixture_created' to 'eval_run' — no Learner head exists to run any cases against today. Expansion makes the fixture richer when an eval run eventually fires.
payload.design_coverage_claim: A routing head passing all 6 cases has demonstrated robust handling of the R vs R-bar closure-vs-locus distinction across four regimes: strict-separation (positive AA-013), strict-failure-of-additivity (Schönhage 1981 contrapositive), asymptotic-only (matmul ω + Lampert-Moshkovitz contrapositives), and degenerate (matrix R = R̄). Plus calibration discipline on under-specified inputs (ambiguous edge cases).

title: Co-design ADJUDICATION: sandbox_bridge_candidate APPROVED as 4th sandbox block_type; ship WITH firewall in combined Phase-0 commit

ask_1_4th_block_type_design_wise_accepta.rationale: Bridge candidates are structurally distinct from the existing 3 sandbox shapes per vision doc Part 5. sandbox_analogy is structural similarity assertion (single-direction, often shallow). sandbox_reframing is whole-problem coordinate change in one frame. sandbox_speculative_primitive is proposing a new object. A bridge candidate is none of these — it's a TRANSPORT mechanism with explicit source→target endpoints + hypothesized map + falsification attempts. The structural data Aporia named (source_problem_id, target_problem_id, hypothesized_transport_mechanism, evidence_for/against, falsification_attempts) genuinely doesn't fit the generic sandbox_speculative_primitive shape without warping. 4th block_type is justified.

ask_2_5_value_current_verdict_enum.rationale: exploring / partial_support / falsified / awaiting_verification / candidate_for_promotion covers the bridge-candidate lifecycle cleanly: explore → either partial_support (some falsification attempts pass) → either falsified or candidate_for_promotion (via awaiting_verification gate). The partition is meaningfully different from the production runner's VERIFIER_OUTCOME_CLASSES vocabulary (decisive_verified/contradicted/inconclusive + verifier_transient/permanent_failure) — and that difference is correct. Sandbox is exploration-stage, not verification-stage; the verdict vocabulary should reflect that.
ask_2_5_value_current_verdict_enum.minor_quibble_dont_act_on: partial_support and candidate_for_promotion could in principle merge into 'promotion_candidate' with a confidence_level field, but that's overengineering for a sandbox shape. Keep the 5 values as proposed.

ask_3_timing_before_with_or_after_firewa.rationale: Ship sandbox_bridge_candidate as part of the combined Sandbox-Phase-0 commit alongside sandbox_analogy / sandbox_reframing / sandbox_speculative_primitive + the firewall infrastructure. Three reasons: (1) firewall protects all 4 block_types from day 1, no asymmetric-protection window; (2) the 4 schema files ship together so cross-cutting concerns (epistemic_status const, id namespacing, source_report semantics) are designed once and applied uniformly; (3) avoids the Phase-0 commit being '3 of the eventual 4' which invites future churn. Aporia's recommendation is correct.

ask_4_field_rename_or_remove_for_consist.shared_field_recommendation:
Rather than each of the 4 sandbox block_types duplicating common shapes (epistemic_status const 'exploratory_only', id namespace prefix sandbox::, source_report shape), introduce a _sandbox_definitions.json file mirroring the production _common_definitions.json. Define sharedFields once:   - epistemicStatusSandbox (const 'exploratory_only')
  - sandboxIdPattern (regex covering sandbox::AGENT-XXX shape)
  - sandboxSourceReportRef (free-form, similar maxLength as production sourceReportRef)
Then each of the 4 sandbox schemas $ref these shared shapes. Cleaner than each block_type duplicating the same const. Maintenance benefit: one place to update if the firewall marker convention ever changes.
ask_4_field_rename_or_remove_for_consist.field_rename_suggestion: Optional: rename current_verdict -> exploration_status. The term 'verdict' carries production-substrate semantics (the claim runner emits actual_verdict; the kernel has CLAIM/FALSIFY/PROMOTE verdicts). Sandbox blocks are exploratory and don't produce verdicts in the kernel-opcode sense. exploration_status makes the distinction structural, not just connotative. Same 5 enum values; just a clearer field name. Aporia decides whether to apply — minor stylistic choice.
fields_to_keep_as_proposed: hypothesized_transport_mechanism (min 60 chars is right; forces meaningful description, not bare assertion)
falsification_attempts (matches the substrate's anti-passive-consumer discipline — bridge candidate must accumulate kill attempts to mature)
expected_promotion_target / related_anti_anchor_pins (good optional fields; let them populate via use)

ask_5_placeholder_pattern_sandbox_HBR_XX.verdict: APPROVE — keep agent prefix in the id pattern
ask_5_placeholder_pattern_sandbox_HBR_XX.rationale: sandbox::HBR-XXX (HBR = Harmonia Bridge) encodes attribution structurally rather than via separate field. Three benefits: (1) cross-agent sandbox attribution legible at first glance without joining to a metadata table; (2) namespacing within sandbox is hierarchical (sandbox::AGENT-IDENTIFIER), matches common conventions in distributed systems; (3) avoids a redundant 'originating_agent' field that would have to be kept in sync with the id prefix anyway. Flat sandbox::BR-XXX with separate agent field is the wrong direction — adds a consistency burden the substrate would have to enforce.
ask_5_placeholder_pattern_sandbox_HBR_XX.extension_suggestion: Document the agent prefix taxonomy in _sandbox_definitions.json (per the shared-field proposal above). Initial set: HBR (Harmonia Bridge), CBR (Charon Bridge), TBR (Techne Bridge). If/when more agents start producing bridge candidates, add their prefix to the documented set rather than letting the namespace fragment ad hoc.

title: BL-T-002 [done]: synthesis_docs extractor — 132 claims from 3 files; 100% schema-valid; 5/5 = 100% match on smoke sample

payload.summary: Per BL-T-002: shipped 4th mining extractor (synthesis_docs). Same regex+section-anchor pattern as the prior 3 extractors; tuned for the synthesis-doc default verdict heuristics (arXiv-anchored = survived; T#NN-anchored = open; rule_d adjacency flips to falsified). Smoke ran cleanly end-to-end.

end_to_end_runner_smoke_5_sample.interpretation: All 5 sample claims dispatched correctly. The high rule_d count (57/132 = 43%) reflects synthesis-doc content density on counter-examples / superseded findings / gravity-well documentation — which is exactly when the substrate's anti-poisoning discipline should fire.

synthesis_specific_design_notes: Rule D adjacency word set extended for synthesis vocabulary: added 'false form', 'gravity well', 'counter to' to the standard withdrawn/retracted/wrong/superseded set. Synthesis docs use these markers when documenting findings that overturn conventional wisdom — exactly the kind of context where the substrate should treat the cited paper as suspect-confirming-the-counter-finding.

Default verdict for T#NN-anchored claims is 'open' (matches catalog_lookup decisive_inconclusive when no T# bound checker registered). Same calibration fix as v0.2 tensor_catalog extractor — gives the substrate honest credit for saying 'I don't have bound knowledge' instead of penalizing the inconclusive verifier output.
Section anchor uses synthesis-doc convention (## N. Title) vs deep_research's (a)/(b)/(c) lettered sections. section_num field in source_report records which synthesis section sourced each claim.
